metadata.name: ww-stack
metadata.description: A sortable drag-and-drop stack/list element that renders repeated item cards with optional header and footer content.
features: Drag-and-drop between stacks with a shared group
Custom header, repeated item, and footer slots
Optional custom drag handle CSS class
Move, add, and remove events with updated item lists
Readonly isDragging variable while drag is active

properties.name: group
properties.description: Drag-and-drop group name. Stacks with the same group can exchange items.

properties.name: items
properties.description: Array or collection of item objects rendered by the itemElement slot.

properties.name: sortable
properties.description: Whether items can be dragged and reordered.

properties.name: customDragHandle
properties.description: Whether dragging must start from an element inside the item that has the handleClass CSS class.

properties.name: handleClass
properties.description: CSS class used as the drag handle when customDragHandle is true.

states.name: readonly
states.description: Applied when the stack is readonly and cannot be reordered.

events.name: item:moved
events.description: Triggered when an item is reordered inside the stack.

payload.updatedList: array of items after the move

events.name: item:added
events.description: Triggered when an item is added from another stack in the same group.

payload.updatedList: array of items after the addition

events.name: item:removed
events.description: Triggered when an item is removed from this stack into another stack in the same group.

payload.updatedList: array of items after the removal

slots.name: itemElement
slots.description: Required repeated card/template rendered once for each item. Inside this slot, use context.item.data for the current item and context.item.index for the current index.

slots.name: headerElement
slots.description: Optional content rendered above the repeated items. In a Kanban board, this commonly displays the stack label from context.item.data.label.

slots.name: footerElement
slots.description: Optional content rendered below the repeated items, often used for an add-card action or summary.

variables.name: isDragging
variables.description: True while an item in the stack is being dragged.

specifications: Use ww-stack for a standalone sortable list or as the stackElement child of ww-kanban.
The items property must resolve to an array. Each item should be an object and should include a stable id field when possible because the internal item key defaults to id.
The itemElement slot is repeated automatically. Do not add your own repeat on itemElement children; bind text and styles directly to context.item.data fields.
Inside itemElement, use context.item.data.<field> or context.item.data?.['field'] to display item properties.
Inside headerElement when used in ww-kanban, context.item.data is the current stack object with label, value, and items fields.
Use the same group value for multiple standalone stacks when items should be draggable between them.
When customDragHandle is true, add the handleClass value as an HTML class on a child element inside itemElement. Otherwise the whole item can start dragging.
Set readonly to true when the list is display-only.
Use item:moved, item:added, and item:removed events to update the source array when drag-and-drop should persist.

examples.description: Stack item with a custom drag handle.
